name: Dr Nathan W. Hill
title: Reader in Tibetan and Historical Linguistics
department: School of Languages, Cultures and Linguistics
expertise_topics: Nathan W. Hill's research focuses on Tibetan literature and Tibeto-Burman
Sino-Tibetan historical linguistics. In particular he has published on Old Tibetan descriptive linguistics
and the typology of evidential systems
biography: Nathan W. Hill has taught courses in historical linguistics and well as Tibetan language and history. He was educated at the Catlin Gabel School and Harvard University and has also studied in France, Nepal, Tibet and Japan. He came to SOAS in 2008 after teaching at Harvard University and Universität Tübingen. Nathan is able to supervise PhD projects on Tibetan literature and history in the Department of China and Inner Asia, as well as PhD projects on historical, descriptive and corpus linguistics, in particular with reference to Tibetan or other Tibeto-Burman/Sino-Tibetan languages, in the Department of Linguistics.
research_interests: Nathan W. Hill's research focuses on Tibetan literature and Tibeto-Burman; Sino-Tibetan historical linguistics. In particular he has published on Old Tibetan descriptive linguistics; Tibetan corpus linguistics; Tibeto-Burman reconstruction and comparative linguistics; and the typology of evidential systems
